title: lomatium ambiguum subsp. leptocarpum
intro: Lomatium ambiguum subsp. leptocarpum, also known as Lomatium leptocarpum or Great Basin lomatium, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family, Apiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiviral, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its volatile oils and alkaloids. The plant is said to have numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Lomatium ambiguum subsp. leptocarpum is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in traditional rituals and ceremonies.
